data.description:
## Why
Compaction now preserves folded event sources as `*.retired` (invariant I1, epic adept-hedge-stain / b306) instead of deleting them. Retention is currently **permanent** (accepted storage tradeoff); the tombstones accumulate on the tickets branch over time.

## What
Add a branch-wide GC sweep that removes `*.retired` files provably past **causal stability** — no clone can still be mid-reconvergence against the pre-compaction events. Must honor the concurrency doctrine (I1-I9): never remove a `.retired` whose companion `.json` could still be reintroduced by an in-flight rebase/merge.

## Scope
- A `compact-gc` (or `compact --gc`) path listing `*.retired` under the tracker, removing only those past a configurable causal-stability horizon.
- docs/concurrency.md I1: promote the 'follow-up' note to implemented behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GC removes only `*.retired` past the causal-stability horizon; recent ones kept.
- [ ] Two-clone test: a not-yet-observed compaction's `.retired` files are NOT GC'd (no resurrection window).
- [ ] fsck + replay remain clean after a GC sweep.
- [ ] docs/concurrency.md I1 updated from 'follow-up' to 'implemented'.
data.title: Branch-wide *.retired garbage-collection sweep (past causal stability)
